hypothesis: If an LLM-based agent framework (MatAgent) iteratively combines large language model reasoning with specialized external tools (memory, periodic table, and knowledge base) and property feedback from structure prediction and evaluation models, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies and generate more valid, unique, and novel inorganic material compositions compared to tool-free LLM variants and purely diffusion-based generative models (MatterGen), because the structured retrieval and iterative refinement enable the agent to leverage chemical domain knowledge and prior experimental outcomes to guide compositional search more effectively.
